title: His Own Pawn, His Own Cage
setup: Black's king sits in the corner with the **h7** pawn blocking his only escape, while your king on **f7** locks the rest.
hint: March the rook all the way up — the {{h7}} pawn is doing your job for you.
explanation:
## The Idea

A pure **back-rank corner mate**. Your king on **f7** chokes off g7 and g8, and the Black h7 pawn finishes the cage.

Play {{Ra8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{g8}} — covered by your king on **f7**
- {{g7}} — also covered by your king on **f7**
- {{h7}} — blocked by Black's own pawn
- The king cannot capture the rook (h8 to a8 is half a board away) and nothing can interpose

> **Takeaway:** A king on the 7th rank one file away from the enemy king is the *standard cage* for K+R vs K. Once the king is boxed, the rook delivers the killing blow from any safe square on the back rank.
